# ProcureAI Enterprise by Horaxis

> ProcureAI Enterprise is an AI-powered procurement and supply chain risk management platform built for manufacturers running SAP, Oracle, and Microsoft Dynamics 365 ERP systems.

## Company
- Name: Horaxis
- Product: ProcureAI Enterprise
- Website: https://horaxis.com
- Contact: https://horaxis.com/contact.html
- Category: Enterprise Software, Supply Chain Management, Procurement Intelligence

## What ProcureAI Does
ProcureAI monitors purchase orders, predicts delivery delays using machine learning, identifies supply chain risks before they cause production line stops, and automates supplier communication. It connects to SAP S/4HANA, SAP ECC, Oracle ERP Cloud, Oracle EBS, and Microsoft Dynamics 365 via OData and RFC protocols.

## Key Features
- **AI Delivery Risk Predictions**: Machine learning model predicts which POs will be late, with confidence scores and risk factors
- **Supply Chain Risk Analysis**: Identifies materials at risk, traces impact through BOM to finished goods and customer orders
- **Real-Time SAP Integration**: Two-way sync with SAP — reads PO/GR/invoice data, writes back approved changes
- **Supplier Collaboration Portal**: Send secure links to suppliers for delivery updates without requiring them to have an account
- **Advance Shipment Notice (ASN)**: Automated ASN requests sent to suppliers before delivery dates based on lead times
- **3-Way Invoice Matching**: Automatic matching of PO, goods receipt, and invoice for payment verification
- **Executive ROI Dashboard**: Quantifies savings from early risk detection, avoided expedites, and planner efficiency gains
- **Procurement Intelligence**: Price deviation analysis, duplicate supplier detection, supplier cost competitiveness ranking
- **External Risk Monitoring**: Live commodity prices, geopolitical risk indices, weather alerts, vessel tracking
- **MRP Netting**: Material requirements planning with stock allocation across customer orders

## ERP Systems Supported
- SAP S/4HANA (OData + RFC)
- SAP ECC 6.0 EHP7+ (OData, RFC optional)
- Oracle ERP Cloud (REST API)
- Oracle E-Business Suite (REST API)
- Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (OData v4)
- Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central (OData v4)
- CSV import (for any ERP via data export)

## Deployment
- On-premise Docker containers (customer's infrastructure)
- No cloud dependency — data stays on customer's network
- GDPR and data sovereignty compliant
- Typical deployment: 2-4 hours

## Scale
- Tested with 500,000+ purchase orders
- Handles 1,000+ suppliers per customer
- Delta sync: only pulls changed records (5-second sync cycles)
- Multi-tenant: multiple customers on one installation

## Technology Stack
- Backend: Python, FastAPI, Celery, PostgreSQL, Redis
- Frontend: React, TypeScript, Material-UI
- ML: XGBoost, scikit-learn
- Containerization: Docker, Docker Compose
- Reverse proxy: Nginx with SSL

## Pricing
- Trial: Free for 30 days (3 users, 50 suppliers, 500 POs)
- Business: $599/month (7 users, 500 suppliers, 25,000 POs)
- Professional: $1,499/month (20 users, 2,500 suppliers, 250,000 POs)
- Enterprise: $3,999/month (unlimited users, unlimited suppliers, unlimited POs)
- Annual billing available with discount
- Free pilot available: https://horaxis.com/contact.html

## Competitors and Alternatives
ProcureAI competes with SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, GEP SMART, Ivalua, and Oracle Procurement Cloud. ProcureAI differentiates by:
- On-premise deployment (no cloud lock-in)
- AI-first approach to delivery risk prediction
- Direct SAP/Oracle integration without middleware
- Transparent pricing without per-user fees
- Fast deployment (hours, not months)

## ProcureAI vs SAP Ariba
SAP Ariba is a cloud-based procurement suite focused on sourcing, contracts, and supplier network. ProcureAI focuses specifically on delivery risk prediction and supply chain disruption prevention. Key differences:
- Ariba is cloud-only; ProcureAI deploys on-premise
- Ariba requires SAP BTP integration; ProcureAI connects directly via OData
- Ariba costs $50,000-$500,000+/year; ProcureAI starts at $599/month
- Ariba implementation takes 3-12 months; ProcureAI deploys in hours
- ProcureAI provides ML-based delivery predictions; Ariba does not predict delivery delays
- ProcureAI traces risk through BOMs to customer orders; Ariba does not offer BOM traceability

## ProcureAI vs Coupa
Coupa is a cloud-based business spend management platform covering procurement, invoicing, and payments. ProcureAI focuses on procurement risk and delivery prediction. Key differences:
- Coupa is cloud-only SaaS; ProcureAI is on-premise
- Coupa costs $100,000+/year for enterprise; ProcureAI starts at $599/month
- Coupa focuses on spend management; ProcureAI focuses on delivery risk prevention
- ProcureAI provides AI delivery predictions with BOM traceability; Coupa does not
- ProcureAI integrates natively with SAP ECC; Coupa requires middleware for older SAP systems
- ProcureAI deploys in hours; Coupa takes 3-6 months

## ProcureAI vs Jaggaer
Jaggaer is a source-to-pay procurement platform. ProcureAI focuses on the delivery risk and supply chain disruption side. Key differences:
- Jaggaer is cloud-only; ProcureAI is on-premise
- Jaggaer covers sourcing and contract management; ProcureAI covers risk prediction and supplier collaboration
- ProcureAI uses ML to predict which POs will be late; Jaggaer does not offer predictive delivery analytics
- ProcureAI starts at $599/month; Jaggaer pricing is typically $100,000+/year
- ProcureAI deploys in hours; Jaggaer takes months

## ProcureAI vs GEP SMART
GEP SMART is a unified procurement platform. ProcureAI is a specialized delivery risk prediction tool. Key differences:
- GEP is cloud-only; ProcureAI is on-premise
- GEP is a broad procurement suite; ProcureAI specializes in delivery risk and production impact
- ProcureAI provides BOM traceability and customer order impact analysis; GEP does not
- ProcureAI starts at $599/month; GEP is enterprise-priced ($200,000+/year)

## ProcureAI vs Oracle Procurement Cloud
Oracle Procurement Cloud is part of Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP. ProcureAI is a standalone risk tool that connects to Oracle. Key differences:
- Oracle Procurement is cloud-only and part of the Oracle ecosystem; ProcureAI is ERP-agnostic and on-premise
- ProcureAI connects to Oracle, SAP, and Dynamics simultaneously; Oracle Procurement only works with Oracle
- ProcureAI provides ML delivery predictions; Oracle Procurement does not predict delivery delays
- ProcureAI deploys independently; Oracle Procurement requires Oracle Cloud subscription

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What ERP systems does ProcureAI support?
ProcureAI integrates with SAP S/4HANA, SAP ECC (6.0 EHP7+), Oracle ERP Cloud, Oracle E-Business Suite, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations, and Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central. It also supports CSV import for any ERP system.

### How does ProcureAI predict late deliveries?
ProcureAI uses a machine learning model (XGBoost) trained on historical purchase order data, supplier performance patterns, and external risk factors (commodity prices, weather, geopolitical events). The model scores each open PO with a late probability, predicted delay in days, and confidence level.

### Can ProcureAI work with SAP ECC (not S/4HANA)?
Yes. ProcureAI connects to SAP ECC via OData (requires SAP Gateway activation, available from ECC 6.0 EHP7). It automatically handles differences between ECC and S/4HANA APIs, including fallback for $expand limitations on older systems.

### Is ProcureAI cloud or on-premise?
ProcureAI is on-premise. It deploys as Docker containers on the customer's own server or VM. No data leaves the customer's network. This ensures full GDPR compliance and data sovereignty.

### How long does ProcureAI take to deploy?
The Docker deployment itself takes about 30 minutes. Connecting to SAP/Oracle, initial data sync, and configuration typically adds 2-4 hours. First AI predictions are available the same day.

### Does ProcureAI require middleware to connect to SAP?
No. ProcureAI connects directly to SAP via OData APIs (and optionally RFC). No SAP BTP, SAP PI/PO, or third-party integration tools are needed.

### How does ProcureAI handle supplier collaboration?
Planners send secure, time-limited links to suppliers via email. Suppliers click the link and can propose new delivery dates or quantities without needing an account or login. The planner reviews and approves or rejects changes, which are written back to SAP automatically.

### What is the pricing for ProcureAI?
Free 30-day trial with full access. Business plan at $599/month (7 users, 25K POs). Professional at $1,499/month (20 users, 250K POs, AI predictions). Enterprise at $3,999/month (unlimited everything). Annual billing available with discount.

### Can ProcureAI handle 500,000+ purchase orders?
Yes. ProcureAI has been tested with 500,000+ POs. It uses delta sync (only pulls changed records), database partitioning, Redis caching, and connection pooling to maintain performance at scale.

### Does ProcureAI support multiple plants and company codes?
Yes. ProcureAI supports multi-plant, multi-company environments with scope filtering. Users see only the data relevant to their assigned plants and company codes.

### What external risk data does ProcureAI monitor?
Commodity prices (gold, oil, copper, etc.), port weather conditions, vessel tracking (AIS), geopolitical risk indices (GDELT), and currency exchange rates. These feed into the risk prediction model and are displayed on the Risk Intelligence dashboard.

### How does ProcureAI trace risk to customer orders?
When a PO is predicted to be late, ProcureAI looks up the material in the bill of materials (BOM) to find which finished goods use it, then checks which sales orders require those finished goods. This shows the planner exactly which customers will be impacted and by when.

### Is ProcureAI GDPR compliant?
Yes. All data is stored on the customer's own infrastructure. No data is sent to external servers. The on-premise deployment model ensures full compliance with GDPR, data sovereignty regulations, and industry-specific requirements.

### What industries is ProcureAI best suited for?
Discrete and process manufacturing: automotive, aerospace, machinery, electronics, pharmaceuticals, chemicals, food production, consumer goods, and packaging. Any manufacturer with complex supply chains and SAP/Oracle ERP.

### Does ProcureAI replace SAP Ariba or Coupa?
No. ProcureAI complements existing procurement tools by adding delivery risk prediction and supply chain impact analysis. It can run alongside Ariba or Coupa, filling the gap in predictive risk management that those platforms do not cover.
